name: database-fundamentals
description: Auto-invoke when reviewing schema design, database queries, ORM usage, or migrations. Enforces normalization, indexing awareness, query optimization, and migration safety.
capabilities: Review schema design for normalization and data type choices
Check queries for N+1 problems and missing pagination
Recommend indexes for frequently queried columns
Verify migration safety and rollback options
Identify SQL injection vulnerabilities in string concatenation
author: DanielPodolsky
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill with no executable code. Static findings are false positives caused by database terminology triggering security pattern matching. The content_hash field, backtick characters in SQL code examples, and terms like 'hash' (normalization) are incorrectly flagged as security issues. This skill only provides educational database best practices.
valueStatement: Database mistakes cause slow applications and data loss. This skill provides comprehensive review checklists for schema design, queries, ORM patterns, and migrations.
userTitle: Review database code with best practices
limitations: Does not execute queries or connect to databases
Does not generate migration files or schema definitions
Cannot test query performance on live databases

useCases.title: Review ORM model definitions
useCases.description: Check foreign key relationships, data types, and proper indexing on ORM models

useCases.title: Learn query optimization
useCases.description: Understand why SELECT * and N+1 queries cause problems and how to fix them

useCases.title: Validate migration safety
useCases.description: Ensure migrations can be rolled back and will not cause data loss

promptTemplates.title: Schema review
promptTemplates.prompt: Review this database schema for normalization issues, missing primary keys, and improper data types

promptTemplates.title: Query optimization
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze these queries for performance issues including missing indexes and N+1 problems

promptTemplates.title: Migration safety
promptTemplates.prompt: Check if this migration can be safely rolled back without data loss

promptTemplates.title: ORM patterns
promptTemplates.prompt: Review this ORM code for best practices including eager loading and parameterized queries

bestPractices: Always use parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection attacks
Add indexes for columns used in WHERE, JOIN, and ORDER BY clauses
Test migrations on production-like data before deployment
antiPatterns: Using SELECT * instead of specifying required columns
Writing migrations that cannot be rolled back

faq.question: Which database systems are supported?
faq.answer: This skill covers general SQL principles that apply to PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, and other relational databases.

faq.question: Does this work with NoSQL databases like MongoDB?
faq.answer: Some concepts like indexing and query optimization apply, but schema normalization and foreign keys are SQL-specific.

faq.question: Can this generate migration files?
faq.answer: No, this skill reviews existing code and migrations. It does not generate or execute database schema changes.

faq.question: Is data safe when using this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill only reviews text/code. It never connects to databases or executes queries.

faq.question: What if queries still run slowly after applying recommendations?
faq.answer: Use EXPLAIN ANALYZE on your database to identify specific bottlenecks. Complex queries may need query planning or caching.

faq.question: How is this different from a linter?
faq.answer: Linters check syntax. This skill reviews design decisions like schema structure, relationships, and migration strategy.
